page: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Daldv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 8 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU is equipped with 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and SHA_NI. This configuration is paired with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is provided via a 472 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D8alds_v6 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests and the top 10% for Geekbench text processing. Multi-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores falling within the average middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth remains average across both small cached and large uncached read/write operations. Cryptographic and compression tests, such as gzip and bzip3, also align with average tier expectations. For machine learning workloads, the server achieves average text generation speeds on small 135M and medium 7B models, though prompt processing on 7B models is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D8alds_v6 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU, which limits its utility for memory-intensive databases or large-scale data processing. However, this resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The inclusion of a 472 GB local disk provides fast local scratch space without requiring external storage attachments. This server is best suited for single-threaded tasks, web serving, software compilation, and lightweight containerized applications.
description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores), 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 472 GB local disk. The server exhibits top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% in stress-ng single-core benchmarks, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall in the average tier. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, it presents a clear tradeoff: it is highly efficient for CPU-bound, single-threaded tasks but constrained for memory-heavy applications. This instance is suited for web serving, software compilation, and lightweight containerized workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an 8-vCPU server featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 16 GB RAM, and a 472 GB local disk. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for compute-focused workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC server with 16 GB RAM and 472 GB local storage, offering top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: An AMD-powered compute instance featuring high single-core performance, local storage, and a low-memory footprint for efficient CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities
Top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng and text processing benchmarks
Suited for web serving, software compilation, and single-threaded tasks
